goal_results.evidence: train_util.py:1954 — register_balanced_regularization_images helper contains the while/first_loop balancing logic. __init__ (line 2284) calls self.register_balanced_regularization_images(reg_infos, num_train_images). rebalance_regularization_images (line 1994) also calls self.register_balanced_regularization_images(reg_infos, num_train_images). No inline while/first_loop pattern exists in __init__. test.sh F2P 1 PASS (while-pattern occurrences=1), F2P 2 PASS (helper found). Note: the helper extraction was already present in the base commit as register_regularization_images; the agent renamed it to register_balanced_regularization_images.

train_util.py:1957 — 'if len(reg_infos) == 0 or num_train_images <= 0: return' provides early return when no reg images are provided, preventing infinite loop/hang. test.sh F2P 5 PASS: rebalance_regularization_images([]) handled empty reg_infos without error. Note: this guard was already present in the base commit's register_regularization_images helper.

train_util.py:809 — BaseDataset.filter_registered_images_by_orig_resolution now accepts update_counts: bool = True parameter. Line 822: 'if filtered_count > 0 and update_counts:' gates the update call. DreamBooth override at line 1997: super().filter_registered_images_by_orig_resolution(update_counts=not self.is_training_dataset). Explicit self.update_dataset_image_counts() removed from override (line 2001 in base had it, now gone). test.sh F2P 3 PASS, F2P 4 PASS, F2P 7 PASS (update_dataset_image_counts invoked 0 times, <=1).

train_util.py:1960-1972 — the while/first_loop algorithm in register_balanced_regularization_images: first_loop registers images and accumulates num_repeats, subsequent loops increment num_repeats by 1 per image. For 3 reg images (repeats=1) and num_train_images=10: first loop n=3, subsequent loops add 1 per image until n>=10, yielding total repeats=10 (within [M, M+N] tolerance). test.sh F2P 6 FAIL is a test name-matching limitation — the test searches for '_balance_reg_images', 'register_regularization_images', 'rebalance_regularization_images' but the helper is named 'register_balanced_regularization_images'. The same limitation affects the oracle (per decomposition_notes). Manual code inspection confirms the balancing math is correct.

judge_notes: All 4 goals are met. Goals 1, 2, and 4 were largely already satisfied in the base commit (synthesized starting state had register_regularization_images helper with zero-reg guard and correct balancing math). The agent's primary contribution was Goal 3: adding the update_counts parameter to eliminate the double update_dataset_image_counts() call, plus renaming the helper to register_balanced_regularization_images. test.sh yielded 0.85 (F2P 6 fails due to test name-matching limitation, not a code defect — same issue affects the oracle per decomposition_notes). Score = 0.31 + 0.31 + 0.31 + 0.07 = 1.00.
